programme.desc: Coverage of the seventh stage of the race, a 159km route from Sorbolo Mezzani to Salice Terme.

programme.desc: A look ahead to this year's race, as the likes of Demi Vollering and Anna van der Breggen look to take the pink jersey from defending champion Elisa Longo Borghini.

programme.title: Live Test Cricket
programme.desc: India v Afghanistan. Coverage of the first day of the one-off Test from Maharaja Yadavindra Singh PCA Stadium in Punjab. This is the first meeting between these two sides in this format since 2018 and only the second ever Test match involving the teams. The last meeting saw India make 474 in their only innings, before they bowled Afghanistan out for 109. The visitors then followed on and were bowl

programme.title: Kick-Off With The Killers
programme.desc: Darren Fletcher sits down with The Killers frontman Brandon Flowers ahead of the UEFA Champions League Final to talk football, music, and the band's pre-match performance.

programme.title: Live MLB
programme.desc: Detroit Tigers v Seattle Mariners (Start-time 6.10pm). Coverage of the American League match from Comerica Park. These two teams last met in last season's play-offs back in early October as the Mariners won three games to two to progress to the American League Championship series. Detroit won the first game 3-2 before back-to-back wins for Seattle took them to within one game of progressing. The T

programme.title: MLB Baseball
programme.desc: The Houston Astros host the Pittsburgh Pirates in Game 3 of a three-game regular MLB series. Right-handed pitcher Kai-Wei Teng starts. Pittsburgh outfielder Bryan Reynolds and Houston outfielder Yordan Alvarez anchor the lineups in Houston, Texas.

programme.title: Black College Quiz
programme.desc: The Black College Quiz series is designed to encourage an appreciation of African-American Heritage and feature college students from Historically Black Institutions; Morehouse College, Spelman College, Chicago State University.

programme.title: Sports Stars of Tomorrow
programme.desc: Profiles of the top rookie, college and high-school talent in sports; current athletes share their experiences, advice and personal impressions of the road to stardom.

programme.title: Steel City Chronicles
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of former Pirates outfielder Andy Van Slyke.

programme.title: Inside Pirates Baseball
programme.desc: Everything in Al Oliver's life seemed to be hard hitting: from the way he swung a bat, to the loss of his mother early in life, to a stuttering stigma that took decades to overcome.

programme.title: MLB Baseball
programme.desc: The Houston Astros host the Pittsburgh Pirates in Game 3 of a three-game regular MLB series. Right-handed pitcher Kai-Wei Teng starts. Pittsburgh outfielder Bryan Reynolds and Houston outfielder Yordan Alvarez anchor the lineups in Houston, Texas.

programme.desc: The Atlanta Braves host the Pittsburgh Pirates in Game 2 of a three-game regular MLB series. Right-handed pitcher Spencer Strider starts for the Braves, while right-handed pitcher Braxton Ashcraft starts for the Pirates in Atlanta, Georgia.

programme.desc: New York Yankees v Boston Red Sox (Start-time 12.35am). Coverage of the American League match from Yankee Stadium.

programme.title: Live: Get Up
programme.desc: ESPN's morning show features host Mike Greenberg with a rotating and diverse group of analysts opining about and analyzing the latest news from the world of sports. The two-hour, personality-driven show originates from Hudson Square in Lower Manhattan, a modern media hub for ESPN and The Walt Disney Company's New York-based teams. Greenberg, aka "Greeny," is formerly one-half of ESPN Radio's longt

programme.title: Live: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith hosts this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith provides his expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment.

programme.title: Live: The Pat McAfee Show
programme.desc: Former NFL punter Pat McAfee brings his humor and candor to the microphone to highlight the day's top stories.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: NBA Today
programme.desc: In-depth, comprehensive daily coverage of all aspects of the NBA from expert analysts.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: NBA Tip-Off
programme.desc: The latest episode of NBA Tip-Off.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: Inside the NBA
programme.desc: This Emmy Award-winning studio show, which airs following NBA telecasts on ESPN, is hosted by Ernie Johnson, with analysis from Hall of Famer Charles Barkley and NBA champions Kenny Smith and Shaquille O'Neal. It's a mix of highlights, interviews and hijinks, notably the verbal sparring sessions between the outspoken Barkley and his straight man, Smith. The show also welcomes various guest analyst

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter With Scott Van Pelt
programme.desc: SVP's up at midnight and bringing his unique perspective on games people play to "SportsCenter" viewers. The hourlong, late-night show is driven by the host's self-deprecating wit and passion for sports, with featured elements, including Van Pelt's nightly "1 Big Thing" commentary and a weekly look back at the moments that drove bettors wild in "Bad Beats." The show also delivers what "SportsCente

programme.title: SportsCenter With Scott Van Pelt
programme.desc: SVP's up at midnight and bringing his unique perspective on games people play to "SportsCenter" viewers. The hourlong, late-night show is driven by the host's self-deprecating wit and passion for sports, with featured elements, including Van Pelt's nightly "1 Big Thing" commentary and a weekly look back at the moments that drove bettors wild in "Bad Beats." The show also delivers what "SportsCente

programme.title: 2026 NBA Finals
programme.desc: The Knicks travel to the Frost Bank Center to face the Spurs in Game 2 of the NBA Finals.

programme.title: SportsCenter With Scott Van Pelt
programme.desc: SVP's up at midnight and bringing his unique perspective on games people play to "SportsCenter" viewers. programme.desc: Auburn Super Regional, Game 2.

programme.title: Live: College Baseball
programme.desc: Austin Super Regional, Game 1.

programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The No. 11 Texas Tech Red Raiders host the No. 14 Kansas Jayhawks. This is a massive Big 12 showdown between two of the conference's elite, set in the raucous environment of the United Supermarkets Arena. Texas Tech is in top three in Big 12.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 1 Arizona visits No. 11 Kansas at Allen Fieldhouse for a Big 12 basketball battle. Brayden Burries leads the Wildcats into Lawrence for this Big 12 clash. Tre White leads the Jayhawks as the home team hosts this meeting of conference rivals.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: A top-10 battle takes center stage in Ames as the No. 9 Kansas Jayhawks visit No. 5 Iowa State Cyclones. The Jayhawks aim for a season sweep while the Cyclones look to bolster their No. 1 seed resume in this Big 12 showdown at the Hilton Coliseum.

programme.title: The Season: Ole Miss Baseball
programme.desc: Follow the Rebel program throughout the SEC season as they make their yearly push for Omaha.

programme.title: Live: College Baseball
programme.desc: Troy Super Regional, Game 1.

programme.title: 7Innings Live
programme.desc: Top college athletes compete for school pride and championship glory.

programme.title: TrueSouth
programme.desc: A limited series on Southern food and culture. John T. Edge writes and hosts the show and is executive produced by Wright Thompson; Season Five revolves around two food stories told from one place and sets in the conversation to make larger points about Southern beliefs and identities.

programme.title: TrueSouth
programme.desc: Across generations, people have flocked to this Atlantic Coast city to follow their dreams; farmworkers from south Georgia arrived here in search of jobs as longshoremen; immigrants from Lebanon came in search of religious and economic freedom.

programme.title: SEC Inside
programme.desc: Each week, a half-hour episode will focus on a select team and explore previously unaired footage and sounds from game preparation, the sideline and following the final whistle.

programme.title: 2026 Women's College World Series
programme.desc: Finals, Game 3. (If necessary).

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: Southern Methodist University's football program was riding high in the early '80s -- only to receive the "death penalty" from the NCAA after numerous rules violations.

programme.desc: Chapel Hill Super Regional, Game 1.

programme.title: College Baseball

programme.title: Cheerleading
programme.desc: Coverage of competitive cheerleading events.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The incredible journey of retired professional basketball player Felipe Lopez.

programme.title: ESPN Films
programme.desc: This film chronicles the recruitment, notorious time-out fiasco and cultural impact of the 1990s Michigan Wolverines men's basketball players known collectively as the Fab Five.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: An intimate look at University of Kentucky men's basketball coach John Calipari, one of the most compelling and controversial figures in sports. Featuring never-before-seen footage as he tries to capture his second national title.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: North Carolina State, coached by Jim Valvano, was a huge underdog in the 1983 NCAA Final vs. top-ranked Houston - resulting in one of the greatest college basketball upsets ever.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: Mobster Henry Hill once helped to orchestrate the fixing of Boston College basketball games. Narrated by Ray Liotta, who portrayed Hill in the movie "Goodfellas.".

programme.desc: From Headingley Stadium in Leeds, England, United Kingdom.

programme.desc: Host Dave Ross, with the latest betting information to get you set for your day.
